links.title: Agentic Critical Training

primary_link.title: Agentic Critical Training

papers.summary: Training large language models (LLMs) as autonomous agents often begins with imitation learning, but it only teaches agents what to do without understanding why: agents never contrast successful actions against suboptimal alternatives and thus lack awareness of action quality. Recent approaches attempt to address this by introducing self-reflection supervision derived from contrasts between expert and alternative actions. However, the training paradigm fundamentally remains imitation learning: the model imitates pre-constructed reflection text rather than learning to reason autonomously. We propose Agentic Critical Training (ACT), a reinforcement learning paradigm that trains agents to identify the better action among alternatives. By rewarding whether the model's judgment is correct, ACT drives the model to autonomously develop reasoning about action quality, producing genuine self-reflection rather than imitating it. Across three challenging agent benchmarks, ACT consistently improves agent performance when combined with different post-training methods. It achieves an average improvement of 5.07 points over imitation learning and 4.62 points over reinforcement learning. Compared to approaches that inject reflection capability through knowledge distillation, ACT also demonstrates clear advantages, yielding an average improvement of 2.42 points. Moreover, ACT enables strong out-of-distribution generalization on agentic benchmarks and improves performance on general reasoning benchmarks without any reasoning-specific training data, highlighting the value of our method. These results suggest that ACT is a promising path toward developing more reflective and capable LLM agents.
papers.summary_zh: 將大型語言模型 (LLMs) 訓練為自主代理通常始於 imitation learning，但這種方法只教導代理做什麼，卻不理解為什麼：代理從不將成功的行為與次優的替代方案進行對比，因此缺乏對 action quality 的意識。最近的方法試圖透過引入基於專家行為與替代行為對比的 self-reflection supervision 來解決此問題。然而，其訓練範式本質上仍是 imitation learning：模型模仿預先構建的反思文本，而非學習自主推理。我們提出 Agentic Critical Training (ACT)，這是一種 reinforcement learning 範式，旨在訓練代理識別替代方案中較好的行為。透過獎勵模型判斷的正確性，ACT 驅使模型自主發展對 action quality 的推理能力，產生真實的 self-reflection，而非模仿。在三個具挑戰性的 agent benchmarks 上，ACT 結合不同的 post-training methods 時，持續改進代理的性能。它比 imitation learning 平均提升 5.07 分，比 reinforcement learning 平均提升 4.62 分。與透過 knowledge distillation 注入反思能力的方法相比，ACT 也展現出明顯優勢，平均提升 2.42 分。此外，ACT 在 agentic benchmarks 上實現了強大的 out-of-distribution generalization，並在沒有任何特定推理訓練資料的情況下提高了 general reasoning benchmarks 的性能，突顯了我們方法的價值。這些結果表明 ACT 是發展更具反思性和能力的 LLM 代理的 promising path。

links.title: How Far Can Unsupervised RLVR Scale LLM Training?

primary_link.title: How Far Can Unsupervised RLVR Scale LLM Training?

papers.summary: Unsupervised reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (URLVR) offers a pathway to scale LLM training beyond the supervision bottleneck by deriving rewards without ground truth labels. Recent works leverage model intrinsic signals, showing promising early gains, yet their potential and limitations remain unclear. In this work, we revisit URLVR and provide a comprehensive analysis spanning taxonomy, theory and extensive experiments. We first classify URLVR methods into intrinsic versus external based on reward sources, then establish a unified theoretical framework revealing that all intrinsic methods converge toward sharpening the model's initial distribution This sharpening mechanism succeeds when initial confidence aligns with correctness but fails catastrophically when misaligned. Through systematic experiments, we show intrinsic rewards consistently follow a rise-then-fall pattern across methods, with collapse timing determined by model prior rather than engineering choices. Despite these scaling limits, we find intrinsic rewards remain valuable in test-time training on small datasets, and propose Model Collapse Step to measure model prior, serving as a practical indicator for RL trainability. Finally, we explore external reward methods that ground verification in computational asymmetries, showing preliminary evidence they may escape the confidence-correctness ceiling. Our findings chart boundaries for intrinsic URLVR while motivating paths toward scalable alternatives.
papers.summary_zh: 帶有可驗證獎勵的無監督強化學習 (Unsupervised reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards, URLVR) 提供了一條途徑，透過在沒有 ground truth labels 的情況下導出獎勵，將 LLM 訓練擴展到超越 supervision bottleneck 的範圍。最近的研究利用模型 intrinsic signals，顯示出有希望的早期收益，但其潛力與局限性仍不明確。在這項工作中，我們重新審視 URLVR，並提供涵蓋 taxonomy、theory 和大量實驗的全面分析。我們首先根據 reward sources 將 URLVR 方法分為 intrinsic 和 external 兩類，然後建立一個統一的 theoretical framework，揭示所有 intrinsic methods 都會收斂於 sharpening 模型的 initial distribution。當 initial confidence 與 correctness 一致時，這種 sharpening mechanism 會成功；但當兩者不一致時，則會災難性地失敗。透過系統性實驗，我們展示了 intrinsic rewards 在不同方法中始終遵循 rise-then-fall pattern，且 collapse timing 由 model prior 決定，而非 engineering choices。儘管存在這些 scaling limits，我們發現 intrinsic rewards 在 small datasets 上的 test-time training 中仍具價值，並提出了 Model Collapse Step 來衡量 model prior，作為 RL trainability 的實用指標。最後，我們探索了將 verification 建立在 computational asymmetries 上的 external reward methods，顯示初步證據表明它們可能突破 confidence-correctness ceiling。我們的發現為 intrinsic URLVR 劃定了界限，同時激發了通往 scalable alternatives 的路徑。

links.title: PIRA-Bench: A Transition from Reactive GUI Agents to GUI-based Proactive Intent Recommendation Agents

primary_link.title: PIRA-Bench: A Transition from Reactive GUI Agents to GUI-based Proactive Intent Recommendation Agents

papers.summary: Current Graphical User Interface (GUI) agents operate primarily under a reactive paradigm: a user must provide an explicit instruction for the agent to execute a task. However, an intelligent AI assistant should be proactive, which is capable of anticipating user intentions directly from continuous visual inputs, such as mobile or desktop screenshots, and offering timely recommendations without explicit user prompting. Transitioning to this proactive paradigm presents significant challenges. Real-world screen activity is rarely linear; it consists of long-horizon trajectories fraught with noisy browsing, meaningless actions, and multithreaded task-switching. To address this gap, we introduce PIRA-Bench (Proactive Intent Recommendation Agent Benchmark), a novel benchmark for evaluating multimodal large language models (MLLMs) on continuous, weakly-supervised visual inputs. Unlike reactive datasets, PIRA-Bench features complex trajectories with multiple interleaved intents and noisy segments with various user profile contexts, challenging agents to detect actionable events while fitting to user preferences. Furthermore, we propose the PIRF baseline, a memory-aware, state-tracking framework that empowers general MLLMs to manage multiple task threads and handle misleading visual inputs. PIRA-Bench serves as an initial step toward robust and proactive GUI-based personal assistants.
papers.summary_zh: 目前的 Graphical User Interface (GUI) 代理主要在 reactive paradigm 下運作：用戶必須提供明確的指令，代理才能執行任務。然而，一個智能的 AI 助理應該是 proactive 的，能夠直接從連續的 visual inputs（例如 mobile 或 desktop screenshots）預測用戶意圖，並在沒有 explicit user prompting 的情況下提供及時的 recommendations。轉向這種 proactive paradigm 帶來了重大挑戰。現實世界的螢幕活動很少是線性的；它由充滿 noisy browsing、meaningless actions 和 multithreaded task-switching 的 long-horizon trajectories 組成。為了解決這個差距，我們引入了 PIRA-Bench (Proactive Intent Recommendation Agent Benchmark)，這是一個用於評估 multimodal large language models (MLLMs) 在連續、weakly-supervised visual inputs 上的 novel benchmark。與 reactive datasets 不同，PIRA-Bench 具有複雜的 trajectories，其中包含多個 interleaved intents 和具有各種 user profile contexts 的 noisy segments，挑戰代理在適應用戶偏好的同時檢測 actionable events。此外，我們提出了 PIRF baseline，這是一個 memory-aware、state-tracking framework，它使通用 MLLMs 能夠管理多個 task threads 並處理 misleading visual inputs。PIRA-Bench 是邁向 robust 且 proactive 的 GUI-based personal assistants 的初步一步。

papers.title_zh: PIRA-Bench：從響應式 GUI 代理到基於 GUI 的主動意圖推薦代理的轉變

papers.summary: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting improves LLM accuracy on complex tasks but often increases token usage and inference cost. Existing "Budget Forcing" methods reducing cost via fine-tuning with heuristic length penalties, suppress both essential reasoning and redundant filler. We recast efficient reasoning as a lossy compression problem under the Information Bottleneck (IB) principle, and identify a key theoretical gap when applying naive IB to transformers: attention violates the Markov property

links.title: FVG-PT: Adaptive Foreground View-Guided Prompt Tuning for Vision-Language Models

primary_link.title: FVG-PT: Adaptive Foreground View-Guided Prompt Tuning for Vision-Language Models

papers.summary: CLIP-based prompt tuning enables pretrained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to efficiently adapt to downstream tasks. Although existing studies have made significant progress, they pay limited attention to changes in the internal attention representations of VLMs during the tuning process. In this paper, we attribute the failure modes of prompt tuning predictions to shifts in foreground attention of the visual encoder, and propose Foreground View-Guided Prompt Tuning (FVG-PT), an adaptive plug-and-play foreground attention guidance module, to alleviate the shifts. Concretely, FVG-PT introduces a learnable Foreground Reliability Gate to automatically enhance the foreground view quality, applies a Foreground Distillation Compensation module to guide visual attention toward the foreground, and further introduces a Prior Calibration module to mitigate generalization degradation caused by excessive focus on the foreground. Experiments on multiple backbone models and datasets show the effectiveness and compatibility of FVG-PT. Codes are available at: https://github.com/JREion/FVG-PT

radar.summary: Few-shot adaptation of vision-language models (VLMs) like CLIP typically relies on learning textual prompts matched to global image embeddings. Recent works extend this paradigm by incorporating local image-text alignment to capture fine-grained visual cues, yet these approaches often select local regions independently for each prompt, leading to redundant local feature usage and prompt overlap. We propose SOT-GLP, which introduces a shared sparse patch support and balanced optimal transport all
radar.summary_zh: 像 CLIP 這樣的 vision-language models (VLMs) 的 few-shot 適應通常依賴於學習與 global image embeddings 匹配的 textual prompts。最近的工作透過結合 local image-text alignment 來捕捉 fine-grained visual cues，擴展了這一範式，然而這些方法通常為每個 prompt 獨立選擇局部區域，導致冗餘的局部特徵使用和 prompt 重疊。我們提出了 SOT-GLP，它引入了一個共享的 sparse patch support 和平衡的 optimal transport。
radar.title: Local-Global Prompt Learning via Sparse Optimal Transport
radar.title_zh: 基於稀疏最優傳輸的局部-全域 Prompt 學習

links.title: CORE-Acu: Structured Reasoning Traces and Knowledge Graph Safety Verification for Acupuncture Clinical Decision Support

primary_link.title: CORE-Acu: Structured Reasoning Traces and Knowledge Graph Safety Verification for Acupuncture Clinical Decision Support

radar.summary: Large language models (LLMs) show significant potential for clinical decision support (CDS), yet their black-box nature -- characterized by untraceable reasoning and probabilistic hallucinations -- poses severe challenges in acupuncture, a field demanding rigorous interpretability and safety. To address this, we propose CORE-Acu, a neuro-symbolic framework for acupuncture clinical decision support that integrates Structured Chain-of-Thought (S-CoT) with knowledge graph (KG) safety verification.
radar.summary_zh: Large language models (LLMs) 在 clinical decision support (CDS) 方面展現出巨大潛力，然而其 black-box 的本質——表現為不可追溯的推理和機率性 hallucinations——在針灸領域構成了嚴峻挑戰，該領域對可解釋性和安全性有嚴格要求。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 CORE-Acu，這是一個用於針灸 clinical decision support 的 neuro-symbolic framework，它將 Structured Chain-of-Thought (S-CoT) 與 knowledge graph (KG) 安全驗證相整合。
radar.title: CORE-Acu: Structured Reasoning Traces and Knowledge Graph Safety Verification for Acupuncture Clinical Decision Support
radar.title_zh: CORE-Acu：用於針灸臨床決策支持的結構化推理軌跡與知識圖譜安全驗證

links.title: Is continuous CoT better suited for multi-lingual reasoning?

primary_link.title: Is continuous CoT better suited for multi-lingual reasoning?

radar.summary: We investigate whether performing reasoning in a continuous latent space leads to more robust multilingual capabilities. We compare Continuous Chain-of-Thought (using the CODI framework) against standard supervised fine-tuning across five typologically diverse languages: English, Chinese, German, French, and Urdu. radar.summary_zh: 我們研究在 continuous latent space 中執行推理是否能帶來更 robust 的多語言能力。我們將 Continuous Chain-of-Thought（使用 CODI 框架）與標準的 supervised fine-tuning 進行比較，涵蓋五種類型學上多樣的 languages：English、Chinese、German、French 和 Urdu。我們在 GSM8k 和 CommonsenseQA 上的實驗證明，continuous reasoning 在 low-resource languages 上顯著優於 explicit reasoning，尤其是在 zero-shot 設定中。

links.title: Gender Bias in MT for a Genderless Language: New Benchmarks for Basque

primary_link.title: Gender Bias in MT for a Genderless Language: New Benchmarks for Basque

radar.summary: Large language models (LLMs) and machine translation (MT) systems are increasingly used in our daily lives, but their outputs can reproduce gender bias present in the training data. Most resources for evaluating such biases are designed for English and reflect its sociocultural context, which limits their applicability to other languages. This work addresses this gap by introducing two new datasets to evaluate gender bias in translations involving Basque, a low-resource and genderless language.
radar.summary_zh: 大型語言模型 (LLMs) 和 machine translation (MT) 系統在我們的日常生活中被越來越廣泛地使用，但它們的輸出可能會重現訓練資料中存在的 gender bias。大多數用於評估此類 bias 的資源都是為 English 設計的，並反映其社會文化背景，這限制了它們對其他 languages 的適用性。這項工作透過引入兩個新 datasets 來解決這個問題，用於評估涉及 Basque (一種 low-resource 且 genderless 的 language) 翻譯中的 gender bias。
radar.title: Gender Bias in MT for a Genderless Language: New Benchmarks for Basque
radar.title_zh: 無性別語言 MT 中的 Gender Bias：針對 Basque 的新 Benchmarks

primary_link.title: Tiny Autoregressive Recursive Models

radar.summary: Tiny Recursive Models (TRMs) have recently demonstrated remarkable performance on ARC-AGI, showing that very small models can compete against large foundation models through a two-step refinement mechanism that updates an internal reasoning state $z$ and the predicted output $y$. Naturally, such refinement is of interest for any predictor; it is therefore natural to wonder whether the TRM mechanism could be effectively re-adopted in autoregressive models. However, TRMs cannot be simply compared
radar.summary_zh: Tiny Recursive Models (TRMs) 最近在 ARC-AGI 上展現了卓越的性能，表明非常小的模型可以透過兩步細化機制（更新內部推理狀態 $z$ 和預測輸出 $y$）與大型基礎模型競爭。自然地，這種細化對於任何預測器都具有吸引力；因此，很自然地會思考 TRM 機制是否可以在自迴歸模型中有效地重新採用。然而，TRMs 無法簡單地進行比較...
radar.title: Tiny Autoregressive Recursive Models
radar.title_zh: 微型自迴歸遞歸模型

links.title: OfficeQA Pro: An Enterprise Benchmark for End-to-End Grounded Reasoning

primary_link.title: OfficeQA Pro: An Enterprise Benchmark for End-to-End Grounded Reasoning

top5.summary: We introduce OfficeQA Pro, a benchmark for evaluating AI agents on grounded, multi-document reasoning over a large and heterogeneous document corpus. The corpus consists of U.S. Treasury Bulletins spanning nearly 100 years, comprising 89,000 pages and over 26 million numerical values. OfficeQA Pro consists of 133 questions that require precise document parsing, retrieval, and analytical reasoning across both unstructured text and tabular data. Frontier LLMs including Claude Opus 4.6, GPT-5.4, and Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview achieve less than 5% accuracy on OfficeQA Pro when relying on parametric knowledge, and less than 12% with additional access to the web. When provided directly with the document corpus, frontier agents still struggle on over half of questions, scoring 34.1% on average. We find that providing agents with a structured document representation produced by Databricks' ai_parse_document yields a 16.1% average relative performance gain across agents. We conduct additional ablations to study the effects of model selection, table representation, retrieval strategy, and test-time scaling on performance. Despite these improvements, significant headroom remains before agents can be considered reliable at enterprise-grade grounded reasoning.
top5.summary_zh: 我們引入了 OfficeQA Pro，一個用於評估 AI 代理在大型異構文件語料庫上進行接地、多文件推理的基準測試。該語料庫包含近 100 年的 U.S. Treasury Bulletins，總計 89,000 頁和超過 2,600 萬個數值。OfficeQA Pro 包含 133 個問題，這些問題需要對非結構化文本和表格數據進行精確的文件解析、檢索和分析推理。前沿 LLM，包括 Claude Opus 4.6、GPT-5.4 和 Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview，在依賴參數化知識時，OfficeQA Pro 上的準確率不到 5%；即使額外存取網絡，其準確率也低於 12%。當直接提供文件語料庫時，前沿代理在超過一半的問題上仍然表現不佳，平均得分為 34.1%。我們發現，為代理提供由 Databricks 的 ai_parse_document 生成的結構化文件表示，可以為代理帶來平均 16.1% 的相對性能提升。我們進行了額外的消融實驗，以研究模型選擇、表格表示、檢索策略和測試時間擴展對性能的影響。儘管取得了這些改進，但在代理能夠在企業級接地推理方面被認為可靠之前，仍有巨大的改進空間。
top5.title: OfficeQA Pro: An Enterprise Benchmark for End-to-End Grounded Reasoning
top5.title_zh: OfficeQA Pro: 一個用於端到端接地推理的企業級基準測試

links.title: Rethinking Attention Output Projection: Structured Hadamard Transforms for Efficient Transformers

primary_link.title: Rethinking Attention Output Projection: Structured Hadamard Transforms for Efficient Transformers

top5.summary: The dense output projection in multi-head attention scales quadratically with model dimension, contributing significantly to parameter count, memory footprint, and inference cost. We propose replacing this projection with a fixed, parameter-free Walsh Hadamard Transform followed by a lightweight learnable affine rescaling, eliminating approximately 25 percent of attention parameters per block while preserving global cross head interaction through an orthogonal, norm-preserving transformation. Ac
top5.summary_zh: 多頭注意力中的密集輸出投影與模型維度呈平方級增長，顯著增加了參數數量、記憶體佔用和推理成本。我們提出用一個固定、無參數的 Walsh Hadamard Transform 取代這個投影，然後進行一個輕量級可學習的仿射重縮放，這消除了每個區塊大約 25% 的注意力參數，同時通過正交、範數保留變換保留了全局跨頭部交互。Ac
top5.title: Rethinking Attention Output Projection: Structured Hadamard Transforms for Efficient Transformers

primary_link.title: Adaptive Loops and Memory in Transformers: Think Harder or Know More?

top5.summary: Chain-of-thought (CoT) prompting enables reasoning in language models but requires explicit verbalization of intermediate steps. Looped transformers offer an alternative by iteratively refining representations within hidden states.
